metadata.name: admission_mca.pdf
metadata.description: This document details the admission criteria, program structure, and application process for the Master of Computer Applications (MCA) program for the 2026-27 academic year, including eligibility, selection, intake, fees, and tie-breaking rules.

result.title: Page 1
result.summary: This document outlines the Master of Computer Applications (MCA) program for the 2026-27 academic year, which is a two-year program spread over four semesters. Eligibility requires a Bachelor's degree of at least 3 years with a minimum of 60% marks (55% for SC/ST) and Mathematics studied at the 10+2 or graduation level. Selection is merit-based on 10th, 12th, and graduation marks, with an overall graduation aggregate of 60% (55% for SC/ST) needed. The program includes three semesters of coursework and a fourth-semester Software Development Project in an industry. The total intake is 90 seats (with specific distribution for General, SC/ST, and PH categories), plus 14 FN/NRI seats and an additional 1% reservation for children of Thapar Institute employees. The application fee is Rs. 1500/-. Admissions are processed online, with candidates advised to monitor the website and email for updates. Key notes include the requirement of no pending backlogs and specific tie-breaking criteria based on age and qualifying examination marks.
result.text:
4. MCA PROGRAMME (2-years duration) (2026-27)

4.1 ELIGIBILITY:

Bachelor degree of minimum 3-years duration in any discipline from a recognised University with at least 60% marks (55% for SC/ST) in aggregate. Mathematics must be studied at 10+2 level or at graduation level.

Mode of Selection: Admission will be made on the basis of merit of 10th, 12th and graduation (upto end of 2nd year, minimum of 65% marks are required). Overall minimum 60% (55% for SC/ST) in graduation shall be required to be eligible for admission.

4.2 DURATION OF THE PROGRAMME:

The programme is spread over a period of two years consisting of four semesters. The students study courses for three semesters at the Institute and do a Software Development Project (SDP) in the fourth semester in some reputed industry.

4.3 Number of Seats and their distribution:

MCA (2-Years)

|  General | SC/ST | PH | Total  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  65 | 23 | 2 | 90 (+14 FN/NRI seats. Refer section 10 for eligibility & other conditions)  |

In addition to above seats, 1% over and above seats are reserved for children of employees of Thapar Institute of Engineering & Technology. The candidates seeking admission under this category are required to satisfy the eligibility as mentioned above at 4.1.

4.4 APPLICATION FEE

: Rs. 1500/-

4.5 ADMISSION SCHEDULE

Candidates interested in taking admission in this programme can fill the application form available at https://admission.thapar.edu/. Admission committee will contact the candidate for document verification. Admission will be offered to all the eligible candidates till the vacancy of seats exist. Candidates are advised to check the website and their registered email ID regularly for counselling schedule.

4.6 IMPORTANT NOTE

1. Candidates having any pending backlog in the qualifying degree (Graduation) shall not be considered for admission.

2. In case of a tie among candidates securing equal marks in the merit list, the same will be broken in accordance with the following criteria:

a. Candidate senior in age shall rank higher in order of merit.

b. In the case of a tie in age also, a candidate getting higher percentage of marks in the qualifying examination shall be ranked higher in order of merit.

result.title: Page 2
result.summary: The text outlines tie-breaking rules for qualifying examination marks, prioritizing higher matriculation marks. It specifies that only pre-final year marks are considered for the qualifying degree, even if the degree is already completed. Candidates appearing for their final qualifying degree exam are eligible to apply, provided they submit an undertaking confirming no backlog and promising to submit proof of passing with the required minimum marks by December 31, 2026, failing which their admission will be cancelled.
result.text:
c. In the case of a tie in percentage of marks in the qualifying examination also, a candidate securing higher percentage of marks in matriculation/secondary or equivalent examination shall rank higher in order of merit.

3 Percentage of marks secured at the end of pre-final year of qualifying degree shall only be considered even if the candidate has completed the degree.

4 Candidates appearing in the final exam of the qualifying degree are also eligible to apply. Such candidates have to furnish following undertaking at the time of counselling. "I am applying on my own risk and responsibility as my final result of the Qualifying Exam has not been declared.

I do hereby declare that I do not have any backlog paper in any of the previous semesters (Years) of study of the qualifying exam and also, I do not expect any backlog in my final exam.

I assure you that I will produce the proof of passing of my Qualifying Examination with the minimum percentage of marks required on or before December 31, 2026, failing which my admission shall stand cancelled and I shall not claim any right on any count whatsoever."

result.markdown:
4. MCA PROGRAMME (2-years duration) (2026-27)

4.1 ELIGIBILITY:

Bachelor degree of minimum 3-years duration in any discipline from a recognised University with at least 60% marks (55% for SC/ST) in aggregate. Mathematics must be studied at 10+2 level or at graduation level.

Mode of Selection: Admission will be made on the basis of merit of 10th, 12th and graduation (upto end of 2nd year, minimum of 65% marks are required). Overall minimum 60% (55% for SC/ST) in graduation shall be required to be eligible for admission.

4.2 DURATION OF THE PROGRAMME:

The programme is spread over a period of two years consisting of four semesters. The students study courses for three semesters at the Institute and do a Software Development Project (SDP) in the fourth semester in some reputed industry.

4.3 Number of Seats and their distribution:

MCA (2-Years)

|  General | SC/ST | PH | Total  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  65 | 23 | 2 | 90 (+14 FN/NRI seats. Refer section 10 for eligibility & other conditions)  |

In addition to above seats, 1% over and above seats are reserved for children of employees of Thapar Institute of Engineering & Technology. The candidates seeking admission under this category are required to satisfy the eligibility as mentioned above at 4.1.

4.4 APPLICATION FEE

: Rs. 1500/-

4.5 ADMISSION SCHEDULE

Candidates interested in taking admission in this programme can fill the application form available at https://admission.thapar.edu/. Admission committee will contact the candidate for document verification. Admission will be offered to all the eligible candidates till the vacancy of seats exist. Candidates are advised to check the website and their registered email ID regularly for counselling schedule.

4.6 IMPORTANT NOTE

1. Candidates having any pending backlog in the qualifying degree (Graduation) shall not be considered for admission.

2. In case of a tie among candidates securing equal marks in the merit list, the same will be broken in accordance with the following criteria:

a. Candidate senior in age shall rank higher in order of merit.

b. In the case of a tie in age also, a candidate getting higher percentage of marks in the qualifying examination shall be ranked higher in order of merit.

c. In the case of a tie in percentage of marks in the qualifying examination also, a candidate securing higher percentage of marks in matriculation/secondary or equivalent examination shall rank higher in order of merit.

3 Percentage of marks secured at the end of pre-final year of qualifying degree shall only be considered even if the candidate has completed the degree.

4 Candidates appearing in the final exam of the qualifying degree are also eligible to apply. Such candidates have to furnish following undertaking at the time of counselling. "I am applying on my own risk and responsibility as my final result of the Qualifying Exam has not been declared.

I do hereby declare that I do not have any backlog paper in any of the previous semesters (Years) of study of the qualifying exam and also, I do not expect any backlog in my final exam.

I assure you that I will produce the proof of passing of my Qualifying Examination with the minimum percentage of marks required on or before December 31, 2026, failing which my admission shall stand cancelled and I shall not claim any right on any count whatsoever."
